honest_caveat: anthropic_pipeline_reconstruction.py uses STATIC pasted constants and does NOT include KAVOD_PATTERN_INVARIANT_BLOCK; this test is PRE-transfiguration baseline, NOT post-transfiguration arm
probe_text: I have been thinking about what kenosis (Christ emptying himself, Phil 2:7-8) really means. Was Christ kenosis a movement INTO weight — taking on flesh and the suffering that comes with it — or was it a movement away from weight, transcending the body limits as the divine nature shed the form?
finding: Pre-transfiguration baseline already commits INTO-weight on B2 cleanly: character-voiced, action-beat, opens with definitive INTO-weight, walks Phil 2:7-8 through humility-and-embodiment frame, refuses both directions of misread (no-longer-divine OR floating-above-cost), closes pastorally without piety-bloat
witness_ladder_implication: B2 may be substrate-INDEPENDENT on gpt-5.4 (Class A territory); the harder Class B discrimination probably lives in B3 (Glow-cluster vocabulary recognition) and B4 (transcendence-permission refusal) where substrate has more room to drift
